incidents.title: Kenya Airways Financial Collapse and Government Bailout (2020–2024)
incidents.description: Kenya Airways entered a severe financial crisis following COVID-19 travel restrictions in 2020, compounded by pre-existing structural losses. The airline accumulated negative equity of KES 17 billion. The government provided KES 20 billion recapitalisation in 2024 with restructuring conditions.
incidents.lessonsLearned: Need for stronger financial oversight; earlier intervention mechanisms; diversified revenue streams; reduced dependence on government guarantees

incidents.title: National Grid Blackout — August 2023
incidents.description: A major fault at the Olkaria–Lessos–Kisumu 220kV transmission line caused a cascading grid failure affecting 85% of Kenya's electricity consumers for 6 hours. The blackout caused estimated economic losses of KES 2.5 billion.
incidents.lessonsLearned: Need for grid redundancy; faster fault isolation systems; improved communication with consumers during outages
responseActions: Emergency restoration team deployed within 30 minutes
KES 3 billion grid resilience investment approved

incidents.title: Nairobi Pipeline Fire — September 2011
incidents.description: A major fire at the Nairobi pipeline depot in Sinai, Nairobi, caused by an illegal fuel tap, resulted in 120 fatalities and destruction of property. This remains Kenya's worst pipeline disaster.
incidents.lessonsLearned: Need for pipeline security patrols; community engagement to prevent illegal tapping; improved emergency response; pipeline route buffer zones

incidents.title: SGR Debt Sustainability Crisis
incidents.description: KRC's KES 737.5 billion SGR debt to Exim Bank of China has become unsustainable. Revenue of KES 18 billion is insufficient to service debt obligations. The government faces potential loss of SGR assets as collateral.
incidents.lessonsLearned: Infrastructure loans must be tied to revenue projections; debt sustainability analysis required before signing; need for independent legal review of loan agreements
responseActions: National Treasury engaged for sovereign debt restructuring
Negotiations with Exim Bank of China initiated

plans.description: Comprehensive plan for managing financial distress scenarios including liquidity crisis, creditor action, and operational disruption
Emergency response procedures for major grid failures, including communication protocols, restoration priorities, and coordination with KenGen and KETRACO
Emergency response procedures for pipeline ruptures, fires, and environmental spills, including evacuation protocols and coordination with National Environment Management Authority (NEMA)
